.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:12:  - solution-architecture.md (v1.0.4 — advanced elicitation back-fill with ADRs 013/014/015, Functions 7/8, §9 field-partition table, `feedback` sub-collection, `shopLifecycle` state machine, `amountReceivedByShop` invariant, `previousProjectIds`, `defaultLocale` flag)
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:67:9. **Udhaar vocabulary discipline.** Stories that touch UdhaarLedger use only allowed field names (`recordedAmount`, `acknowledgedAt`, `partialPaymentReferences`, `runningBalance`, `closedAt`, `notes`). Forbidden vocabulary (`interest`, `dueDate`, `lendingTerms`, etc.) is enforced at the security rule layer per ADR-010.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:68:10. **Cloud Function references.** Stories that depend on a Cloud Function name the function from SAD §7 (one of: `killSwitchOnBudgetAlert`, `generateWaMeLink`, `sendUdhaarReminder`, `multiTenantAuditJob`, `firebasePhoneAuthQuotaMonitor`, `joinDecisionCircle`, `mediaCostMonitor`, `shopDeactivationSweep`). *(v1.0.5 patch — inventory extended to 8 functions per SAD v1.0.4 §7.)*
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:69:11. **Project field-partition discipline (v1.0.5 patch per SAD §9 field-partition table).** Repository methods cannot construct a `Project` patch that crosses the customer/operator/system field partition defined in SAD §9. Enforced at compile time via Freezed sealed unions: `ProjectCustomerPatch` touches only customer-owned fields (`occasion`, `unreadCountForCustomer`), `ProjectOperatorPatch` touches only operator-owned fields (`state`, `totalAmount`, `amountReceivedByShop`, `lineItems[]`, `committedAt`/`paidAt`/`deliveredAt`/`closedAt`, `customerDisplayName`, `customerPhone`, `customerVpa`, `decisionCircleId`, `udhaarLedgerId`, `unreadCountForShopkeeper`), `ProjectSystemPatch` is written by Cloud Functions only (`lastMessagePreview`, `lastMessageAt`, `updatedAt` server timestamp). Cross-partition mutations must compose multiple typed patches at a higher layer. Cross-tenant integrity test asserts that no customer-app code path emits an `OperatorPatch` and vice versa. This rule gates any story whose AC writes to a `Project` document.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:467:   - `ProjectOperatorPatch` — fields: `state?`, `totalAmount?`, `amountReceivedByShop?`, `lineItems[]?`, `committedAt?`, `paidAt?`, `deliveredAt?`, `closedAt?`, `customerDisplayName?`, `customerPhone?`, `customerVpa?`, `decisionCircleId?`, `udhaarLedgerId?`, `unreadCountForShopkeeper?`
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:1406:6. The Project's `udhaarLedgerId` field is set
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:1440:5. If `runningBalance` reaches 0, `closedAt: serverTimestamp()` is set
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:1530:| Refs | Brief §9 R16 DPDP exposure, Brief §7 v1.5 scope (pulled to v1), SAD v1.0.4 ADR-013 shop lifecycle state machine, SAD v1.0.4 §5 Shop lifecycle fields, SAD v1.0.4 §7 Function 8 `shopDeactivationSweep`, SAD §6 `shopIsWritable` helper |
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:1533:1. The customer app subscribes to the `Shop` document in real time (already done for branding hot-reload). When `shopLifecycle` transitions from `"active"` to `"deactivating"` or `"deactivated"`, the next screen refresh renders a persistent **top banner in Devanagari** above all other content: `सुनील भैया की दुकान बंद हो रही है — आपका पैसा वापस आ जाएगा, आपका डेटा {N} दिन तक सुरक्षित है` ("Sunil-bhaiya's shop is closing — your money will come back, your data is safe for {N} days") where `{N}` is `(dpdpRetentionUntil - now)` in days.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:1534:2. When `shopLifecycle == "deactivating"`, ALL the current customer's `Project` documents with `state in ['draft', 'negotiating', 'committed']` automatically transition to `state: "paused"` via a Cloud-Function-written `ProjectSystemPatch` (owned by `shopDeactivationSweep`). The customer cannot commit new orders; existing committed orders either complete (if shopkeeper ships before purge window) or refund via offline channels.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:1535:3. Any open `UdhaarLedger` documents for this customer freeze — `sendUdhaarReminder` skips any ledger where the parent shop's `shopLifecycle != "active"` (cross-reference with §7 Function 3 guardrails). Running balance is preserved for audit. The customer's view of the ledger shows `रुका हुआ` ("paused") instead of the remind toggle.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:1536:4. An in-app push notification (FCM) is fired once per customer on the `active → deactivating` transition: title `महत्वपूर्ण सूचना`, body equal to the banner text in AC #1. Per DPDP Act 2023, the notification must land within 7 days — `shopDeactivationSweep` runs daily so this is automatically met.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:1538:6. The banner, the paused-project-state, and the frozen-udhaar rendering are visible to the customer for the full DPDP retention window. When `shopDeactivationSweep` transitions the shop to `purge_scheduled`, the banner text updates: `डेटा {30 - days_elapsed} दिन में हटा दिया जाएगा — export कीजिए`.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:1544:1. Customer is offline when `shopDeactivationSweep` runs: the banner appears on next connectivity (Firestore real-time listener catches up). The 7-day DPDP notification window absorbs up to 7 days of customer offline time; anything beyond that is a bad-faith-but-unavoidable edge case.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:1545:2. Customer has an active Project mid-payment at the `active → deactivating` moment: the UPI deep link still works (the client already has the intent URL). If the UPI transaction completes, `shopDeactivationSweep` on its next run detects the new payment and pauses the Project AFTER the payment, with the payment preserved. The customer is notified via the banner that their order is paused pending shopkeeper action.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:1548:5. DPDP retention window changes mid-lifecycle (Yugma Labs legal tightens from 180 to 90 days): `dpdpRetentionUntil` is editable per shop and the banner text auto-updates on next real-time listener fire.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:1853:1. Udhaar tab in ops app shows a list of all open ledgers (where `closedAt == null` and `runningBalance > 0`)
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:1857:5. Closed ledgers (where `closedAt != null` OR `runningBalance == 0`) are filterable separately
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:2040:4. Burnout warning fires but the operator has no `fcmToken`: Crashlytics event still logs; ops runbook handles out-of-band contact.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:2096:| Refs | SAD v1.0.4 ADR-013 shop lifecycle state machine, SAD v1.0.4 §5 Shop lifecycle fields, SAD v1.0.4 §7 Function 8 `shopDeactivationSweep`, paired with C3.12 (customer side), Brief §9 R16 |
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:2101:3. Tap 2 of 3 requires selecting a reason from a dropdown: `दुकानदार रिटायर हो रहे हैं` ("shopkeeper retiring") | `व्यवसाय बंद हो रहा है` ("business closing") | `अनुबंध समाप्त` ("contract terminated") | `डेटा हटाने का अनुरोध` ("data deletion request"). This maps to the SAD v1.0.4 §5 `Shop.shopLifecycleReason` enum.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:2105:   shopLifecycle: "deactivating"
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:2106:   shopLifecycleChangedAt: serverTimestamp()
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:2107:   shopLifecycleReason: <selected reason>
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:2108:   dpdpRetentionUntil: <now + 180 days ISO timestamp>
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:2110:6. Writing these fields triggers the next overnight run of `shopDeactivationSweep` (SAD v1.0.4 §7 Function 8), which then owns ALL subsequent state transitions and customer notifications (C3.12 customer side). The ops app does NOT directly notify customers — the Cloud Function does it, so the notification cadence is server-controlled and DPDP-compliant.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:2111:7. **Audit trail (v1.0.5).** A write to `shops/{shopId}/audit/{eventId}` records `{event: "shop_deactivation_triggered", triggeredByUid, triggeredAt, reason, dpdpRetentionUntil}`. The audit log is retained beyond the shop's lifecycle per SAD v1.0.4 §7 Function 8 design.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:2112:8. **Reversibility during the deactivating window.** Between `active → deactivating` and `deactivating → deactivated` (next overnight sweep), the bhaiya can UNDO via the same Settings screen. The flow re-shows the same 3-tap confirmation with inverted language (`दुकान फिर से चालू कीजिए` "Reopen shop") and writes `shopLifecycle: "active"`. Outside this window (once sweep has transitioned to `deactivated`), reversal requires Yugma Labs ops intervention (a manual admin-SDK write with legal review — not an in-app flow).
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:2113:9. **Security rule enforcement (v1.0.5 AC).** Firestore security rule for `/shops/{shopId}` requires `isBhaiyaOf(shopId)` for any write that touches `shopLifecycle` or related fields. The rule also guards: once `shopLifecycle != "active"`, the only client-side write allowed on these fields is the `deactivating → active` reversal within the first 24 hours. All other lifecycle transitions are Cloud-Function-owned (admin SDK bypasses security rules).
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:2114:10. Cross-tenant integrity test: a bhaiya of `shop_1` cannot write `shopLifecycle` on `shop_0`; a beta of `shop_1` cannot write it on `shop_1`.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:2121:5. DPDP lawyer later demands a shorter retention window for a specific shop: Yugma ops manually tightens `dpdpRetentionUntil` via admin SDK; the banner on customer side (C3.12) auto-updates.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\implementation-readiness-report.md:37:The 6-phase BMAD back-fill has successfully converged the planning chain through Advanced Elicitation gates that were skipped in the original planning round. Phases 1–5 produced a substantively stronger bundle than v1.0 — 8 new PRD stories closing 9 brief→PRD audit gaps, 3 new SAD ADRs (013–015), 2 new Cloud Functions (7 `mediaCostMonitor`, 8 `shopDeactivationSweep`), 6 new UX interaction patterns, 20 new Hindi voice & tone strings, 8 new bundle widgets + 1 widget extension + 1 new file (`invoice_template.dart`), and 6 new visual mockups in `walking-skeleton.html`.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\implementation-readiness-report.md:109:- **S4.19** → SAD v1.0.4 ADR-013 + SAD §5 Shop lifecycle fields + SAD §7 Function 8 `shopDeactivationSweep` + Brief §9 R16

.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\epics-and-stories.md:527:- **C3.12 ↔ S4.19 (paired)** — must ship together. S4.19 ops write creates the `shopLifecycle: "deactivating"` state that C3.12 customer listener reads. Partial ship breaks the DPDP notification contract.
.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\epics-and-stories.md:614:11. **Project field-partition discipline** *(v1.2 add — per PRD v1.0.5 Standing Rule 11, SAD v1.0.4 §9 field-partition table)*. Repository methods cannot construct a `Project` / `ChatThread` / `UdhaarLedger` patch that crosses the customer/operator/system field partition defined in SAD §9. Enforced at compile time via Freezed sealed unions: `ProjectCustomerPatch` (customer-owned fields only: `occasion`, `unreadCountForCustomer`), `ProjectOperatorPatch` (operator-owned fields only: `state`, `totalAmount`, `amountReceivedByShop`, `lineItems[]`, `committedAt`/`paidAt`/`deliveredAt`/`closedAt`, `customerDisplayName`, `customerPhone`, `customerVpa`, `decisionCircleId`, `udhaarLedgerId`, `unreadCountForShopkeeper`), `ProjectSystemPatch` (Cloud-Function-only writes: `lastMessagePreview`, `lastMessageAt`, `updatedAt` server timestamp). Cross-partition mutations must compose multiple typed patches at a higher layer. The cross-tenant integrity test asserts that no customer-app code path emits an `OperatorPatch` and vice versa. `ChatThread` and `UdhaarLedger` follow the same discipline via `ChatThreadOperatorPatch` / `ChatThreadParticipantPatch` / `ChatThreadSystemPatch` and `UdhaarLedgerOperatorPatch`. **This rule gates any story whose AC writes to a Project, ChatThread, or UdhaarLedger document — enforced by I6.12 in Sprint 1.**

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:988:+- SAD says `shopDeactivationSweep` runs *03:00 IST*; code runs *02:00 IST*.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1011:+| `shopDeactivationSweep` | `0 2 * * *` IST | DPDP state machine: `deactivating → purgeScheduled` (24h grace), `purgeScheduled → purged` (180-day retention; PII anonymization on 7 fields) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1186:+    participant Sweep as shopDeactivationSweep CF (daily 02:00 IST)
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1189:+    SApp->>FS: write /shops/{shopId} shopLifecycle='deactivating'
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1193:+    Sweep->>FS: query shops where shopLifecycle='deactivating'
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1194:+    Sweep->>FS: update shopLifecycle='purgeScheduled', purgeScheduledAt=now
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1197:+    Sweep->>FS: query shops where shopLifecycle='purgeScheduled' AND now > purgeScheduledAt+180d
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1199:+    Sweep->>FS: shopLifecycle='purged'
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1214:+    Bhaiya->>SApp: Open udhaarLedger entry, toggle "याद दिलाऊँ?"

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1412:+1. **Firestore rule** — `hasForbiddenUdhaarFields()` rejects 8 English field names on `udhaarLedger` create + update (lines 112–123, 289–307).
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1559:+- **No reminder on closed ledger** — query filters `closedAt == null`.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1650:+| ADR-013 | Shop lifecycle state machine + DPDP scoped deletion | Accepted (v1.0.4 patch) | `shopLifecycle` enum, `shopIsWritable` rule helper, 180-day retention, sweep CF |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1668:+| **E** | ✅ **RESOLVED** (server side) — `shop_deactivation_sweep.ts` now sends bilingual (Devanagari title/body + concatenated English) FCM on both `deactivating → purgeScheduled` and `purgeScheduled → purged`. Token discovery is best-effort: customer tokens come from `customerFcmToken` fields on udhaar ledger docs (the only place customer tokens currently land); operator tokens come from the operators collection (`fcmToken` field). Per-token failures (stale tokens, etc.) are logged at WARN and counted but don't abort the batch. The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1680:+| **D** | **Cloud Function count drift (9 vs SAD's 8) + 4 cron cadence drifts.** `triggerMarketingRebuild` undocumented; `multiTenantAudit` weekly-Sun in code, daily in SAD; `mediaCostMonitor` daily 11:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor` daily 10:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `shopDeactivationSweep` 02:00 in code, 03:00 in SAD. | Patch SAD §7 to reflect the 9 functions and the actual cron strings. |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2016-p0final4.md:1694:+| **R** | **7 of 9 Cloud Functions have zero unit-test coverage.** Only `kill_switch.test.ts` and `trigger_marketing_rebuild.test.ts` exist. | Author tests for `sendUdhaarReminder`, `shopDeactivationSweep`, `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor`, `mediaCostMonitor`, `multiTenantAudit`, `joinDecisionCircle`, `generateWaMeLink`. Add Firebase emulator harness. |

The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2010-p0final3.md:1694:+| **R** | **7 of 9 Cloud Functions have zero unit-test coverage.** Only `kill_switch.test.ts` and `trigger_marketing_rebuild.test.ts` exist. | Author tests for `sendUdhaarReminder`, `shopDeactivationSweep`, `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor`, `mediaCostMonitor`, `multiTenantAudit`, `joinDecisionCircle`, `generateWaMeLink`. Add Firebase emulator harness. |

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2010-p0final3.md:7500:.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:9691:_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:69:11. **Project field-partition discipline (v1.0.5 patch per SAD §9 field-partition table).** Repository methods cannot construct a `Project` patch that crosses the customer/operator/system field partition defined in SAD §9. Enforced at compile time via Freezed sealed unions: `ProjectCustomerPatch` touches only customer-owned fields (`occasion`, `unreadCountForCustomer`), `ProjectOperatorPatch` touches only operator-owned fields (`state`, `totalAmount`, `amountReceivedByShop`, `lineItems[]`, `committedAt`/`paidAt`/`deliveredAt`/`closedAt`, `customerDisplayName`, `customerPhone`, `customerVpa`, `decisionCircleId`, `udhaarLedgerId`, `unreadCountForShopkeeper`), `ProjectSystemPatch` is written by Cloud Functions only (`lastMessagePreview`, `lastMessageAt`, `updatedAt` server timestamp). Cross-partition mutations must compose multiple typed patches at a higher layer. Cross-tenant integrity test asserts that no customer-app code path emits an `OperatorPatch` and vice versa. This rule gates any story whose AC writes to a `Project` document.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2010-p0final3.md:7503:.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:9694:_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:467:   - `ProjectOperatorPatch` — fields: `state?`, `totalAmount?`, `amountReceivedByShop?`, `lineItems[]?`, `committedAt?`, `paidAt?`, `deliveredAt?`, `closedAt?`, `customerDisplayName?`, `customerPhone?`, `customerVpa?`, `decisionCircleId?`, `udhaarLedgerId?`, `unreadCountForShopkeeper?`
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2010-p0final3.md:7523:.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:9714:_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\epics-and-stories.md:614:11. **Project field-partition discipline** *(v1.2 add — per PRD v1.0.5 Standing Rule 11, SAD v1.0.4 §9 field-partition table)*. Repository methods cannot construct a `Project` / `ChatThread` / `UdhaarLedger` patch that crosses the customer/operator/system field partition defined in SAD §9. Enforced at compile time via Freezed sealed unions: `ProjectCustomerPatch` (customer-owned fields only: `occasion`, `unreadCountForCustomer`), `ProjectOperatorPatch` (operator-owned fields only: `state`, `totalAmount`, `amountReceivedByShop`, `lineItems[]`, `committedAt`/`paidAt`/`deliveredAt`/`closedAt`, `customerDisplayName`, `customerPhone`, `customerVpa`, `decisionCircleId`, `udhaarLedgerId`, `unreadCountForShopkeeper`), `ProjectSystemPatch` (Cloud-Function-only writes: `lastMessagePreview`, `lastMessageAt`, `updatedAt` server timestamp). Cross-partition mutations must compose multiple typed patches at a higher layer. The cross-tenant integrity test asserts that no customer-app code path emits an `OperatorPatch` and vice versa. `ChatThread` and `UdhaarLedger` follow the same discipline via `ChatThreadOperatorPatch` / `ChatThreadParticipantPatch` / `ChatThreadSystemPatch` and `UdhaarLedgerOperatorPatch`. **This rule gates any story whose AC writes to a Project, ChatThread, or UdhaarLedger document — enforced by I6.12 in Sprint 1.**
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2010-p0final3.md:7545:.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:9747:_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\solution-architecture.md:2649:| `decisionCircleId`, `udhaarLedgerId` | Operator | Ops app |

Cross-partition mutations must compose multiple typed patches at a higher layer. Cross-tenant integrity test asserts that no customer-app code path emits an `OperatorPatch` and vice versa. This rule gates any story whose AC writes to a `Project` document.

The cross-tenant integrity test asserts that no customer-app code path emits an `OperatorPatch` and vice versa. `ChatThread` and `UdhaarLedger` follow the same discipline via `ChatThreadOperatorPatch` / `ChatThreadParticipantPatch` / `ChatThreadSystemPatch` and `UdhaarLedgerOperatorPatch`. **This rule gates any story whose AC writes to a Project, ChatThread, or UdhaarLedger document — enforced by I6.12 in Sprint 1.**

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2010-p0final3.md:9721:docs/reviews\codex-20260428-2010-p0final3.md:1668:+| **E** | ✅ **RESOLVED** (server side) — `shop_deactivation_sweep.ts` now sends bilingual (Devanagari title/body + concatenated English) FCM on both `deactivating → purgeScheduled` and `purgeScheduled → purged`. Token discovery is best-effort: customer tokens come from `customerFcmToken` fields on udhaar ledger docs (the only place customer tokens currently land); operator tokens come from the operators collection (`fcmToken` field). Per-token failures (stale tokens, etc.) are logged at WARN and counted but don't abort the batch. The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2010-p0final3.md:9791:docs/reviews\codex-20260428-1937-pass.md:1668:+| **E** | ✅ **RESOLVED** (server side) — `shop_deactivation_sweep.ts` now sends bilingual (Devanagari title/body + concatenated English) FCM on both `deactivating → purgeScheduled` and `purgeScheduled → purged`. Token discovery is best-effort: customer tokens come from `customerFcmToken` fields on udhaar ledger docs (the only place customer tokens currently land); operator tokens come from the operators collection (`fcmToken` field). Per-token failures (stale tokens, etc.) are logged at WARN and counted but don't abort the batch. The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:988:+- SAD says `shopDeactivationSweep` runs *03:00 IST*; code runs *02:00 IST*.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:1011:+| `shopDeactivationSweep` | `0 2 * * *` IST | DPDP state machine: `deactivating → purgeScheduled` (24h grace), `purgeScheduled → purged` (180-day retention; PII anonymization on 7 fields) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:1186:+    participant Sweep as shopDeactivationSweep CF (daily 02:00 IST)
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:1189:+    SApp->>FS: write /shops/{shopId} shopLifecycle='deactivating'
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:1193:+    Sweep->>FS: query shops where shopLifecycle='deactivating'
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:1194:+    Sweep->>FS: update shopLifecycle='purgeScheduled', purgeScheduledAt=now
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:1197:+    Sweep->>FS: query shops where shopLifecycle='purgeScheduled' AND now > purgeScheduledAt+180d
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:1199:+    Sweep->>FS: shopLifecycle='purged'
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:1214:+    Bhaiya->>SApp: Open udhaarLedger entry, toggle "याद दिलाऊँ?"
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:1218:+    Rem->>FS: query udhaarLedger where reminderOptInByBhaiya=true AND closedAt=null

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:9691:_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:69:11. **Project field-partition discipline (v1.0.5 patch per SAD §9 field-partition table).** Repository methods cannot construct a `Project` patch that crosses the customer/operator/system field partition defined in SAD §9. Enforced at compile time via Freezed sealed unions: `ProjectCustomerPatch` touches only customer-owned fields (`occasion`, `unreadCountForCustomer`), `ProjectOperatorPatch` touches only operator-owned fields (`state`, `totalAmount`, `amountReceivedByShop`, `lineItems[]`, `committedAt`/`paidAt`/`deliveredAt`/`closedAt`, `customerDisplayName`, `customerPhone`, `customerVpa`, `decisionCircleId`, `udhaarLedgerId`, `unreadCountForShopkeeper`), `ProjectSystemPatch` is written by Cloud Functions only (`lastMessagePreview`, `lastMessageAt`, `updatedAt` server timestamp). Cross-partition mutations must compose multiple typed patches at a higher layer. Cross-tenant integrity test asserts that no customer-app code path emits an `OperatorPatch` and vice versa. This rule gates any story whose AC writes to a `Project` document.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:9694:_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\prd.md:467:   - `ProjectOperatorPatch` — fields: `state?`, `totalAmount?`, `amountReceivedByShop?`, `lineItems[]?`, `committedAt?`, `paidAt?`, `deliveredAt?`, `closedAt?`, `customerDisplayName?`, `customerPhone?`, `customerVpa?`, `decisionCircleId?`, `udhaarLedgerId?`, `unreadCountForShopkeeper?`
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-2006-p0final2.md:9714:_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\epics-and-stories.md:614:11. **Project field-partition discipline** *(v1.2 add — per PRD v1.0.5 Standing Rule 11, SAD v1.0.4 §9 field-partition table)*. Repository methods cannot construct a `Project` / `ChatThread` / `UdhaarLedger` patch that crosses the customer/operator/system field partition defined in SAD §9. Enforced at compile time via Freezed sealed unions: `ProjectCustomerPatch` (customer-owned fields only: `occasion`, `unreadCountForCustomer`), `ProjectOperatorPatch` (operator-owned fields only: `state`, `totalAmount`, `amountReceivedByShop`, `lineItems[]`, `committedAt`/`paidAt`/`deliveredAt`/`closedAt`, `customerDisplayName`, `customerPhone`, `customerVpa`, `decisionCircleId`, `udhaarLedgerId`, `unreadCountForShopkeeper`), `ProjectSystemPatch` (Cloud-Function-only writes: `lastMessagePreview`, `lastMessageAt`, `updatedAt` server timestamp). Cross-partition mutations must compose multiple typed patches at a higher layer. The cross-tenant integrity test asserts that no customer-app code path emits an `OperatorPatch` and vice versa. `ChatThread` and `UdhaarLedger` follow the same discipline via `ChatThreadOperatorPatch` / `ChatThreadParticipantPatch` / `ChatThreadSystemPatch` and `UdhaarLedgerOperatorPatch`. **This rule gates any story whose AC writes to a Project, ChatThread, or UdhaarLedger document — enforced by I6.12 in Sprint 1.**

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1933-clean.md:1645:+| ADR-013 | Shop lifecycle state machine + DPDP scoped deletion | Accepted (v1.0.4 patch) | `shopLifecycle` enum, `shopIsWritable` rule helper, 180-day retention, sweep CF |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1933-clean.md:1663:+| **E** | ✅ **RESOLVED** (server side) — `shop_deactivation_sweep.ts` now sends bilingual (Devanagari title/body + concatenated English) FCM on both `deactivating → purgeScheduled` and `purgeScheduled → purged`. Token discovery is best-effort: customer tokens come from `customerFcmToken` fields on udhaar ledger docs (the only place customer tokens currently land); operator tokens come from the operators collection (`fcmToken` field). Per-token failures (stale tokens, etc.) are logged at WARN and counted but don't abort the batch. The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1933-clean.md:1675:+| **D** | **Cloud Function count drift (9 vs SAD's 8) + 4 cron cadence drifts.** `triggerMarketingRebuild` undocumented; `multiTenantAudit` weekly-Sun in code, daily in SAD; `mediaCostMonitor` daily 11:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor` daily 10:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `shopDeactivationSweep` 02:00 in code, 03:00 in SAD. | Patch SAD §7 to reflect the 9 functions and the actual cron strings. |

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1933-clean.md:7701:  In the `deactivating -> purgeScheduled` path, open udhaar ledgers are closed first and only then `collectCustomerFcmTokens(..., true)` runs. Because that helper filters on `closedAt == null`, it will return an empty set right after the freeze, so customers with open udhaar never receive the intended first deactivation notice. This only shows up when a shop is deactivated with open ledgers, but in that case the new notification path is effectively disabled for customers.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1933-clean.md:7713:  In the `deactivating -> purgeScheduled` path, open udhaar ledgers are closed first and only then `collectCustomerFcmTokens(..., true)` runs. Because that helper filters on `closedAt == null`, it will return an empty set right after the freeze, so customers with open udhaar never receive the intended first deactivation notice. This only shows up when a shop is deactivated with open ledgers, but in that case the new notification path is effectively disabled for customers.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1959-p0final.md:378:+5. **Udhaar khaata is an accounting mirror.** Allowed fields: `recordedAmount`, `acknowledgedAt`, `partialPaymentReferences[]`, `runningBalance`, `closedAt`, `notes`. Forbidden field names (will not exist anywhere): `interest`, `interestRate`, `overdueFee`, `dueDate`, `lendingTerms`, `borrowerObligation`, `defaultStatus`, `collectionAttempt` (ADR-010).

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1959-p0final.md:1011:+| `shopDeactivationSweep` | `0 2 * * *` IST | DPDP state machine: `deactivating → purgeScheduled` (24h grace), `purgeScheduled → purged` (180-day retention; PII anonymization on 7 fields) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1959-p0final.md:1186:+    participant Sweep as shopDeactivationSweep CF (daily 02:00 IST)

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1959-p0final.md:1193:+    Sweep->>FS: query shops where shopLifecycle='deactivating'
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1959-p0final.md:1194:+    Sweep->>FS: update shopLifecycle='purgeScheduled', purgeScheduledAt=now

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1959-p0final.md:1412:+1. **Firestore rule** — `hasForbiddenUdhaarFields()` rejects 8 English field names on `udhaarLedger` create + update (lines 112–123, 289–307).
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1959-p0final.md:1559:+- **No reminder on closed ledger** — query filters `closedAt == null`.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1959-p0final.md:1650:+| ADR-013 | Shop lifecycle state machine + DPDP scoped deletion | Accepted (v1.0.4 patch) | `shopLifecycle` enum, `shopIsWritable` rule helper, 180-day retention, sweep CF |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1959-p0final.md:1668:+| **E** | ✅ **RESOLVED** (server side) — `shop_deactivation_sweep.ts` now sends bilingual (Devanagari title/body + concatenated English) FCM on both `deactivating → purgeScheduled` and `purgeScheduled → purged`. Token discovery is best-effort: customer tokens come from `customerFcmToken` fields on udhaar ledger docs (the only place customer tokens currently land); operator tokens come from the operators collection (`fcmToken` field). Per-token failures (stale tokens, etc.) are logged at WARN and counted but don't abort the batch. The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1959-p0final.md:1680:+| **D** | **Cloud Function count drift (9 vs SAD's 8) + 4 cron cadence drifts.** `triggerMarketingRebuild` undocumented; `multiTenantAudit` weekly-Sun in code, daily in SAD; `mediaCostMonitor` daily 11:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor` daily 10:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `shopDeactivationSweep` 02:00 in code, 03:00 in SAD. | Patch SAD §7 to reflect the 9 functions and the actual cron strings. |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1959-p0final.md:1694:+| **R** | **7 of 9 Cloud Functions have zero unit-test coverage.** Only `kill_switch.test.ts` and `trigger_marketing_rebuild.test.ts` exist. | Author tests for `sendUdhaarReminder`, `shopDeactivationSweep`, `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor`, `mediaCostMonitor`, `multiTenantAudit`, `joinDecisionCircle`, `generateWaMeLink`. Add Firebase emulator harness. |

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1806.md:1403:+1. **Firestore rule** — `hasForbiddenUdhaarFields()` rejects 8 English field names on `udhaarLedger` create + update (lines 112–123, 289–307).
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1806.md:1550:+- **No reminder on closed ledger** — query filters `closedAt == null`.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1806.md:1641:+| ADR-013 | Shop lifecycle state machine + DPDP scoped deletion | Accepted (v1.0.4 patch) | `shopLifecycle` enum, `shopIsWritable` rule helper, 180-day retention, sweep CF |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1806.md:1659:+| **E** | ✅ **RESOLVED** (server side) — `shop_deactivation_sweep.ts` now sends bilingual (Devanagari title/body + concatenated English) FCM on both `deactivating → purgeScheduled` and `purgeScheduled → purged`. Token discovery is best-effort: customer tokens come from `customerFcmToken` fields on udhaar ledger docs (the only place customer tokens currently land); operator tokens come from the operators collection (`fcmToken` field). Per-token failures (stale tokens, etc.) are logged at WARN and counted but don't abort the batch. The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1806.md:1671:+| **D** | **Cloud Function count drift (9 vs SAD's 8) + 4 cron cadence drifts.** `triggerMarketingRebuild` undocumented; `multiTenantAudit` weekly-Sun in code, daily in SAD; `mediaCostMonitor` daily 11:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor` daily 10:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `shopDeactivationSweep` 02:00 in code, 03:00 in SAD. | Patch SAD §7 to reflect the 9 functions and the actual cron strings. |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1806.md:1685:+| **R** | **7 of 9 Cloud Functions have zero unit-test coverage.** Only `kill_switch.test.ts` and `trigger_marketing_rebuild.test.ts` exist. | Author tests for `sendUdhaarReminder`, `shopDeactivationSweep`, `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor`, `mediaCostMonitor`, `multiTenantAudit`, `joinDecisionCircle`, `generateWaMeLink`. Add Firebase emulator harness. |

Token discovery is best-effort: customer tokens come from `customerFcmToken` fields on udhaar ledger docs (the only place customer tokens currently land); operator tokens come from the operators collection (`fcmToken` field). Per-token failures (stale tokens, etc.) are logged at WARN and counted but don't abort the batch. The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1956-p0only.md:1412:+1. **Firestore rule** — `hasForbiddenUdhaarFields()` rejects 8 English field names on `udhaarLedger` create + update (lines 112–123, 289–307).
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1956-p0only.md:1559:+- **No reminder on closed ledger** — query filters `closedAt == null`.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1956-p0only.md:1650:+| ADR-013 | Shop lifecycle state machine + DPDP scoped deletion | Accepted (v1.0.4 patch) | `shopLifecycle` enum, `shopIsWritable` rule helper, 180-day retention, sweep CF |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1956-p0only.md:1668:+| **E** | ✅ **RESOLVED** (server side) — `shop_deactivation_sweep.ts` now sends bilingual (Devanagari title/body + concatenated English) FCM on both `deactivating → purgeScheduled` and `purgeScheduled → purged`. Token discovery is best-effort: customer tokens come from `customerFcmToken` fields on udhaar ledger docs (the only place customer tokens currently land); operator tokens come from the operators collection (`fcmToken` field). Per-token failures (stale tokens, etc.) are logged at WARN and counted but don't abort the batch. The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1956-p0only.md:1680:+| **D** | **Cloud Function count drift (9 vs SAD's 8) + 4 cron cadence drifts.** `triggerMarketingRebuild` undocumented; `multiTenantAudit` weekly-Sun in code, daily in SAD; `mediaCostMonitor` daily 11:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor` daily 10:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `shopDeactivationSweep` 02:00 in code, 03:00 in SAD. | Patch SAD §7 to reflect the 9 functions and the actual cron strings. |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1956-p0only.md:1694:+| **R** | **7 of 9 Cloud Functions have zero unit-test coverage.** Only `kill_switch.test.ts` and `trigger_marketing_rebuild.test.ts` exist. | Author tests for `sendUdhaarReminder`, `shopDeactivationSweep`, `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor`, `mediaCostMonitor`, `multiTenantAudit`, `joinDecisionCircle`, `generateWaMeLink`. Add Firebase emulator harness. |

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1857-rerun.md:6796:.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1857-rerun.md:1659:+| **E** | ✅ **RESOLVED** (server side) — `shop_deactivation_sweep.ts` now sends bilingual (Devanagari title/body + concatenated English) FCM on both `deactivating → purgeScheduled` and `purgeScheduled → purged`. Token discovery is best-effort: customer tokens come from `customerFcmToken` fields on udhaar ledger docs (the only place customer tokens currently land); operator tokens come from the operators collection (`fcmToken` field). Per-token failures (stale tokens, etc.) are logged at WARN and counted but don't abort the batch. The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1857-rerun.md:6839:.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\solution-architecture.md:2974:- (b) Store on a separate `fcmTokens` collection keyed by device ID. Allows pre-auth notification subscription. But: extra collection, extra read, extra security rule.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1857-rerun.md:6840:.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\solution-architecture.md:2978:**🔒 LOCKED — Decision: Option (a). FCM token is stored as a field on the Customer document at `shops/{shopId}/customers/{customerUid}.fcmToken`.** Updated whenever the token rotates (the FCM SDK exposes a token-refresh callback). The `sendUdhaarReminder` function reads it via the customer document. The Firestore schema in §5 already shows this field on the Customer entity. Implementation note for Amelia: handle the token-refresh listener in `lib_core/src/persistence/fcm_token_manager.dart`.

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1857-rerun.md:6894:.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\solution-architecture.md:2974:- (b) Store on a separate `fcmTokens` collection keyed by device ID. Allows pre-auth notification subscription. But: extra collection, extra read, extra security rule.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1857-rerun.md:6895:.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\solution-architecture.md:2978:**🔒 LOCKED — Decision: Option (a). FCM token is stored as a field on the Customer document at `shops/{shopId}/customers/{customerUid}.fcmToken`.** Updated whenever the token rotates (the FCM SDK exposes a token-refresh callback). The `sendUdhaarReminder` function reads it via the customer document. The Firestore schema in §5 already shows this field on the Customer entity. Implementation note for Amelia: handle the token-refresh listener in `lib_core/src/persistence/fcm_token_manager.dart`.

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1857-rerun.md:10135:  `collectCustomerFcmTokens()` reads every document in `udhaarLedger`, and Phase 1 calls it after only the open ledgers were frozen. If a shop has historical closed ledgers, those former customers will still receive the new "settle any open transactions soon" notification even though they have nothing outstanding. This bug only appears during `deactivating -> purgeScheduled`, but it will send false payment reminders to the wrong audience.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1857-rerun.md:10147:  `collectCustomerFcmTokens()` reads every document in `udhaarLedger`, and Phase 1 calls it after only the open ledgers were frozen. If a shop has historical closed ledgers, those former customers will still receive the new "settle any open transactions soon" notification even though they have nothing outstanding. This bug only appears during `deactivating -> purgeScheduled`, but it will send false payment reminders to the wrong audience.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:369:+5. **Udhaar khaata is an accounting mirror.** Allowed fields: `recordedAmount`, `acknowledgedAt`, `partialPaymentReferences[]`, `runningBalance`, `closedAt`, `notes`. Forbidden field names (will not exist anywhere): `interest`, `interestRate`, `overdueFee`, `dueDate`, `lendingTerms`, `borrowerObligation`, `defaultStatus`, `collectionAttempt` (ADR-010).

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:979:+- SAD says `shopDeactivationSweep` runs *03:00 IST*; code runs *02:00 IST*.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1002:+| `shopDeactivationSweep` | `0 2 * * *` IST | DPDP state machine: `deactivating → purgeScheduled` (24h grace), `purgeScheduled → purged` (180-day retention; PII anonymization on 7 fields) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1177:+    participant Sweep as shopDeactivationSweep CF (daily 02:00 IST)
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1180:+    SApp->>FS: write /shops/{shopId} shopLifecycle='deactivating'
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1184:+    Sweep->>FS: query shops where shopLifecycle='deactivating'
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1185:+    Sweep->>FS: update shopLifecycle='purgeScheduled', purgeScheduledAt=now
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1188:+    Sweep->>FS: query shops where shopLifecycle='purgeScheduled' AND now > purgeScheduledAt+180d
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1190:+    Sweep->>FS: shopLifecycle='purged'
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1205:+    Bhaiya->>SApp: Open udhaarLedger entry, toggle "याद दिलाऊँ?"
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1209:+    Rem->>FS: query udhaarLedger where reminderOptInByBhaiya=true AND closedAt=null
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1403:+1. **Firestore rule** — `hasForbiddenUdhaarFields()` rejects 8 English field names on `udhaarLedger` create + update (lines 112–123, 289–307).
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1550:+- **No reminder on closed ledger** — query filters `closedAt == null`.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1641:+| ADR-013 | Shop lifecycle state machine + DPDP scoped deletion | Accepted (v1.0.4 patch) | `shopLifecycle` enum, `shopIsWritable` rule helper, 180-day retention, sweep CF |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1659:+| **E** | ✅ **RESOLVED** (server side) — `shop_deactivation_sweep.ts` now sends bilingual (Devanagari title/body + concatenated English) FCM on both `deactivating → purgeScheduled` and `purgeScheduled → purged`. Token discovery is best-effort: customer tokens come from `customerFcmToken` fields on udhaar ledger docs (the only place customer tokens currently land); operator tokens come from the operators collection (`fcmToken` field). Per-token failures (stale tokens, etc.) are logged at WARN and counted but don't abort the batch. The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1671:+| **D** | **Cloud Function count drift (9 vs SAD's 8) + 4 cron cadence drifts.** `triggerMarketingRebuild` undocumented; `multiTenantAudit` weekly-Sun in code, daily in SAD; `mediaCostMonitor` daily 11:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor` daily 10:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `shopDeactivationSweep` 02:00 in code, 03:00 in SAD. | Patch SAD §7 to reflect the 9 functions and the actual cron strings. |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1919-final.md:1685:+| **R** | **7 of 9 Cloud Functions have zero unit-test coverage.** Only `kill_switch.test.ts` and `trigger_marketing_rebuild.test.ts` exist. | Author tests for `sendUdhaarReminder`, `shopDeactivationSweep`, `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor`, `mediaCostMonitor`, `multiTenantAudit`, `joinDecisionCircle`, `generateWaMeLink`. Add Firebase emulator harness. |

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:378:+5. **Udhaar khaata is an accounting mirror.** Allowed fields: `recordedAmount`, `acknowledgedAt`, `partialPaymentReferences[]`, `runningBalance`, `closedAt`, `notes`. Forbidden field names (will not exist anywhere): `interest`, `interestRate`, `overdueFee`, `dueDate`, `lendingTerms`, `borrowerObligation`, `defaultStatus`, `collectionAttempt` (ADR-010).

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:988:+- SAD says `shopDeactivationSweep` runs *03:00 IST*; code runs *02:00 IST*.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1011:+| `shopDeactivationSweep` | `0 2 * * *` IST | DPDP state machine: `deactivating → purgeScheduled` (24h grace), `purgeScheduled → purged` (180-day retention; PII anonymization on 7 fields) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1186:+    participant Sweep as shopDeactivationSweep CF (daily 02:00 IST)
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1189:+    SApp->>FS: write /shops/{shopId} shopLifecycle='deactivating'
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1193:+    Sweep->>FS: query shops where shopLifecycle='deactivating'
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1194:+    Sweep->>FS: update shopLifecycle='purgeScheduled', purgeScheduledAt=now
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1197:+    Sweep->>FS: query shops where shopLifecycle='purgeScheduled' AND now > purgeScheduledAt+180d
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1199:+    Sweep->>FS: shopLifecycle='purged'
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1214:+    Bhaiya->>SApp: Open udhaarLedger entry, toggle "याद दिलाऊँ?"
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1218:+    Rem->>FS: query udhaarLedger where reminderOptInByBhaiya=true AND closedAt=null
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1412:+1. **Firestore rule** — `hasForbiddenUdhaarFields()` rejects 8 English field names on `udhaarLedger` create + update (lines 112–123, 289–307).
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1559:+- **No reminder on closed ledger** — query filters `closedAt == null`.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1650:+| ADR-013 | Shop lifecycle state machine + DPDP scoped deletion | Accepted (v1.0.4 patch) | `shopLifecycle` enum, `shopIsWritable` rule helper, 180-day retention, sweep CF |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1668:+| **E** | ✅ **RESOLVED** (server side) — `shop_deactivation_sweep.ts` now sends bilingual (Devanagari title/body + concatenated English) FCM on both `deactivating → purgeScheduled` and `purgeScheduled → purged`. Token discovery is best-effort: customer tokens come from `customerFcmToken` fields on udhaar ledger docs (the only place customer tokens currently land); operator tokens come from the operators collection (`fcmToken` field). Per-token failures (stale tokens, etc.) are logged at WARN and counted but don't abort the batch. The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1680:+| **D** | **Cloud Function count drift (9 vs SAD's 8) + 4 cron cadence drifts.** `triggerMarketingRebuild` undocumented; `multiTenantAudit` weekly-Sun in code, daily in SAD; `mediaCostMonitor` daily 11:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor` daily 10:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `shopDeactivationSweep` 02:00 in code, 03:00 in SAD. | Patch SAD §7 to reflect the 9 functions and the actual cron strings. |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1942-p3.md:1694:+| **R** | **7 of 9 Cloud Functions have zero unit-test coverage.** Only `kill_switch.test.ts` and `trigger_marketing_rebuild.test.ts` exist. | Author tests for `sendUdhaarReminder`, `shopDeactivationSweep`, `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor`, `mediaCostMonitor`, `multiTenantAudit`, `joinDecisionCircle`, `generateWaMeLink`. Add Firebase emulator harness. |

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1937-pass.md:1412:+1. **Firestore rule** — `hasForbiddenUdhaarFields()` rejects 8 English field names on `udhaarLedger` create + update (lines 112–123, 289–307).

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1937-pass.md:1650:+| ADR-013 | Shop lifecycle state machine + DPDP scoped deletion | Accepted (v1.0.4 patch) | `shopLifecycle` enum, `shopIsWritable` rule helper, 180-day retention, sweep CF |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1937-pass.md:1668:+| **E** | ✅ **RESOLVED** (server side) — `shop_deactivation_sweep.ts` now sends bilingual (Devanagari title/body + concatenated English) FCM on both `deactivating → purgeScheduled` and `purgeScheduled → purged`. Token discovery is best-effort: customer tokens come from `customerFcmToken` fields on udhaar ledger docs (the only place customer tokens currently land); operator tokens come from the operators collection (`fcmToken` field). Per-token failures (stale tokens, etc.) are logged at WARN and counted but don't abort the batch. The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1937-pass.md:1680:+| **D** | **Cloud Function count drift (9 vs SAD's 8) + 4 cron cadence drifts.** `triggerMarketingRebuild` undocumented; `multiTenantAudit` weekly-Sun in code, daily in SAD; `mediaCostMonitor` daily 11:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor` daily 10:00 in code, every-6h in SAD; `shopDeactivationSweep` 02:00 in code, 03:00 in SAD. | Patch SAD §7 to reflect the 9 functions and the actual cron strings. |
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1937-pass.md:1694:+| **R** | **7 of 9 Cloud Functions have zero unit-test coverage.** Only `kill_switch.test.ts` and `trigger_marketing_rebuild.test.ts` exist. | Author tests for `sendUdhaarReminder`, `shopDeactivationSweep`, `phoneAuthQuotaMonitor`, `mediaCostMonitor`, `multiTenantAudit`, `joinDecisionCircle`, `generateWaMeLink`. Add Firebase emulator harness. |

Per-token failures (stale tokens, etc.) are logged at WARN and counted but don't abort the batch. The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1937-pass.md:7832:.\_bmad-output\planning-artifacts\solution-architecture.md:2411:6. **Rule posture interlock.** The §6 security rule helper `shopIsWritable(shopId)` (added v1.0.4) freezes client writes as soon as `shopLifecycle` leaves `active`, so customers and operators cannot mutate data during the retention or purge windows. Reads continue under the normal rules for the retention window, then are denied once the shop document is deleted.

.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1937-pass.md:10037:  `collectCustomerFcmTokens(..., openOnly: true)` filters on `closedAt == null`, but `UdhaarLedgerRepo.createLedger()` does not create a `closedAt` field for open ledgers. In Firestore, that query skips documents where the field is missing, so the new deactivation notification path will find zero customer tokens for every currently-open ledger and no customer will receive the notice unless another write has explicitly set `closedAt: null` first.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1937-pass.md:10046:  `collectCustomerFcmTokens(..., openOnly: true)` filters on `closedAt == null`, but `UdhaarLedgerRepo.createLedger()` does not create a `closedAt` field for open ledgers. In Firestore, that query skips documents where the field is missing, so the new deactivation notification path will find zero customer tokens for every currently-open ledger and no customer will receive the notice unless another write has explicitly set `closedAt: null` first.
.\docs\reviews\codex-20260428-1949-final2.md:424:+5. **Udhaar khaata is an accounting mirror.** Allowed fields: `recordedAmount`, `acknowledgedAt`, `partialPaymentReferences[]`, `runningBalance`, `closedAt`, `notes`. Forbidden field names (will not exist anywhere): `interest`, `interestRate`, `overdueFee`, `dueDate`, `lendingTerms`, `borrowerObligation`, `defaultStatus`, `collectionAttempt` (ADR-010).

The audit history row now includes a `notificationsSent` count. **Caveat:** until customer-side `getToken()` registration lands (drift MT-2 / §15.2.L), customers without an open udhaar ledger won't receive notifications — the function logs an explicit "no tokens available — skipped silently" line so the gap is visible. | (resolved) | (resolved + MT-2 dependency) |

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command 'Get-Content packages/lib_core/lib/src/repositories/customer_memory_repo.dart' in C:\Alok\Business Projects\Almira-Project

// =============================================================================
// CustomerMemoryRepo â€” Firestore access for
//   /shops/{shopId}/customer_memory/{customerUid}.
//
// OPERATOR-ONLY writes. Customer must NEVER have read or write access.
// Per B1.11 AC #1 + S4.9 ACs.
//
// Write surface:
//   - getMemory(customerUid) â€” fetch existing memory doc (or null).
//   - watchMemory(customerUid) â€” real-time stream.
//   - upsertMemory(customerUid, fields) â€” create-or-update. Auto-save path
//     from S4.9 edit sheet calls this with debounced field changes.
// =============================================================================

import 'package:cloud_firestore/cloud_firestore.dart';
import 'package:logging/logging.dart';

import '../models/customer_memory.dart';
import '../shop_id_provider.dart';

/// Normalize Firestore Timestamp â†’ ISO8601 for Freezed JSON parsing.
Object? _normalizeTimestamp(Object? value) {
  if (value == null) return null;
  if (value is Timestamp) return value.toDate().toIso8601String();
  if (value is DateTime) return value.toIso8601String();
  return value;
}

/// Normalize timestamp fields in raw Firestore map for CustomerMemory.
Map<String, dynamic> _normalizeMemoryTimestamps(Map<String, dynamic> raw) {
  return <String, dynamic>{
    ...raw,
    'firstSeenAt': _normalizeTimestamp(raw['firstSeenAt']),
    'lastSeenAt': _normalizeTimestamp(raw['lastSeenAt']),
  };
}

class CustomerMemoryRepoException implements Exception {
  const CustomerMemoryRepoException(this.code, this.message);
  final String code;
  final String message;
  @override
  String toString() => 'CustomerMemoryRepoException($code): $message';
}

class CustomerMemoryRepo {
  CustomerMemoryRepo({
    required FirebaseFirestore firestore,
    required ShopIdProvider shopIdProvider,
  })  : _firestore = firestore,
        _shopIdProvider = shopIdProvider;

  final FirebaseFirestore _firestore;
  final ShopIdProvider _shopIdProvider;
  static final Logger _log = Logger('CustomerMemoryRepo');

  CollectionReference<Map<String, dynamic>> _collection() => _firestore
      .collection('shops')
      .doc(_shopIdProvider.shopId)
      .collection('customer_memory');

  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  // Reads
  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  Future<CustomerMemory?> getMemory(String customerUid) async {
    final snap = await _collection().doc(customerUid).get();
    if (!snap.exists) return null;
    return CustomerMemory.fromJson(<String, dynamic>{
      ..._normalizeMemoryTimestamps(snap.data()!),
      'customerUid': customerUid,
      'shopId': _shopIdProvider.shopId,
    });
  }

  Stream<CustomerMemory?> watchMemory(String customerUid) =>
      _collection().doc(customerUid).snapshots().map((snap) {
        if (!snap.exists) return null;
        return CustomerMemory.fromJson(<String, dynamic>{
          ..._normalizeMemoryTimestamps(snap.data()!),
          'customerUid': customerUid,
          'shopId': _shopIdProvider.shopId,
        });
      });

  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  // Writes (operator-only)
  // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

  /// Create or update the customer memory document. Uses merge semantics
  /// so partial updates (e.g., only notes changed) don't clobber other fields.
  ///
  /// Edge case #1 from S4.9: memory doc doesn't exist yet â†’ created on first
  /// edit via set-with-merge.
  ///
  /// Edge case #2: two operators edit simultaneously â†’ last write wins. This
  /// is acceptable per PRD ("rare; typically one operator handles
  /// relationships").
  Future<void> upsertMemory({
    required String customerUid,
    String? notes,
    String? relationshipNotes,
    List<PreferredOccasion>? preferredOccasions,
    int? preferredPriceMin,
    int? preferredPriceMax,
  }) async {
    final data = <String, Object?>{
      'shopId': _shopIdProvider.shopId,
      'customerUid': customerUid,
      'lastSeenAt': FieldValue.serverTimestamp(),
    };

    if (notes != null) data['notes'] = notes;
    if (relationshipNotes != null) {
      data['relationshipNotes'] = relationshipNotes;
    }
    if (preferredOccasions != null) {
      data['preferredOccasions'] =
          preferredOccasions.map((e) => e.wireValue).toList();
    }
    // CR #2: always include price fields â€” use FieldValue.delete() to clear
    // when null, so previously-set values don't persist after the operator
    // clears the field.
    data['preferredPriceMin'] =
        preferredPriceMin ?? FieldValue.delete();
    data['preferredPriceMax'] =
        preferredPriceMax ?? FieldValue.delete();

    await _collection().doc(customerUid).set(
      data,
      SetOptions(merge: true),
    );
    _log.info('upsertMemory customerUid=$customerUid');
  }
}

 succeeded in 297ms:
// =============================================================================
// DecisionCircle â€” Freezed model for
//   /shops/{shopId}/decision_circles/{projectId}.
//
// Per SAD Â§5 DecisionCircle (optional schema) + ADR-009.
// Feature-flagged via `decisionCircleEnabled`.
//
// The DecisionCircle is the Pariwar pillar's core primitive â€” it tracks
// which family member is currently looking at the phone and maintains a
// participant history. It is OPTIONAL: deleting it does not affect the
// Project (per ADR-009 AC #5).
// =============================================================================

import 'package:freezed_annotation/freezed_annotation.dart';

part 'decision_circle.freezed.dart';
part 'decision_circle.g.dart';

/// A participant session in the decision circle.
@freezed
class DecisionCircleParticipant with _$DecisionCircleParticipant {
  const factory DecisionCircleParticipant({
    required String sessionId,
    required String personaLabel,
    String? deviceId,
    DateTime? lastSeenAt,
  }) = _DecisionCircleParticipant;

  factory DecisionCircleParticipant.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
      _$DecisionCircleParticipantFromJson(json);
}

/// The DecisionCircle document.
@freezed
class DecisionCircle with _$DecisionCircle {
  const factory DecisionCircle({
    required String projectId,
    required String shopId,
    required String primaryCustomerUid,

    /// Participant sessions â€” each one represents a persona who has
    /// looked at this project on this device.
    @Default(<DecisionCircleParticipant>[])
    List<DecisionCircleParticipant> participants,

    /// The currently active persona label â€” updated when the device-holder
    /// switches personas via P2.2 toggle. Shopkeeper sees this in the
    /// project detail to know who is currently looking.
    String? currentActivePersona,

    required DateTime createdAt,
  }) = _DecisionCircle;

  factory DecisionCircle.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
      _$DecisionCircleFromJson(json);
}

// =============================================================================
// CuratedShortlist â€” Freezed model for /shops/{shopId}/curatedShortlists/{id}.
//
// Schema per SAD Â§5 CuratedShortlist entity + PRD B1.4 + UX Spec v1.1 Â§4.3.
//
// "Sunil-bhaiya ki pasand" â€” the shopkeeper hand-picks a FINITE set of
// SKUs (max 6 per occasion per UX Spec Â§4.3 pushback) for each of six
// occasion categories. The customer browses these instead of an infinite
// product grid. The finiteness IS the feature.
//
// Consumed by:
//   - PRD B1.4 (customer-side shortlist render)
//   - PRD B1.12 (shopkeeper-side "remote control for the finger" curation)
// =============================================================================

import 'package:freezed_annotation/freezed_annotation.dart';

part 'curated_shortlist.freezed.dart';
part 'curated_shortlist.g.dart';

/// The 6 occasion categories the customer browses by. Mirrors PRD B1.4
/// AC #1 + UX Spec v1.1 Â§5.5 rows #3-5. Domain-grounded naming â€” NOT
/// generic "category_1..6" or "collection_a/b/c".
enum ShortlistOccasion {
  /// à¤¶à¤¾à¤¦à¥€ à¤•à¥‡ à¤²à¤¿à¤ â€” for a wedding (highest volume driver per Brief Persona A).
  @JsonValue('shaadi')
  shaadi,

  /// à¤¨à¤ à¤˜à¤° à¤•à¥‡ à¤²à¤¿à¤ â€” for the new home (Persona B: new homeowner).
  @JsonValue('naya_ghar')
  nayaGhar,

  /// à¤¬à¥‡à¤Ÿà¥€ à¤•à¤¾ à¤¨à¤¯à¤¾ à¤˜à¤° â€” for daughter's new home.
  @JsonValue('beti_ka_ghar')
  betiKaGhar,

  /// à¤ªà¥à¤°à¤¾à¤¨à¤¾ à¤¬à¤¦à¤²à¤¨à¥‡ à¤•à¥‡ à¤²à¤¿à¤ â€” to replace the old one (Persona C: replacement
  /// buyer). Honest framing per Maya's reframe.
  @JsonValue('replacement')
  replacement,

  /// à¤¬à¤œà¤Ÿ à¤•à¥‡ à¤…à¤¨à¥à¤¸à¤¾à¤° â€” budget-friendly picks. Never called "cheap" / "à¤¸à¤¸à¥à¤¤à¤¾".
  @JsonValue('budget')
  budget,

  /// à¤²à¥‡à¤¡à¥€à¤œ à¤•à¥‡ à¤²à¤¿à¤ â€” for female customers / their specific preferences.
  @JsonValue('ladies')
  ladies,
}

/// The curated shortlist document.
///
/// The `skuIdsInOrder` field is the load-bearing state â€” the shopkeeper's
/// ORDER matters. Drag-to-reorder in S4.12 / B1.12 writes the new order
/// back, and the customer sees SKUs in exactly the order the shopkeeper
/// intended (not alphabetized, not sorted by price).
@freezed
class CuratedShortlist with _$CuratedShortlist {
  /// Construct a curated shortlist.
  const factory CuratedShortlist({
    required String shortlistId,
    required String shopId,
    required ShortlistOccasion occasion,

    /// Devanagari title per UX Spec Â§5.5 #3-5. Source-of-truth language.
    required String titleDevanagari,

    /// English title for the toggle locale. Mirrors the Devanagari.
    required String titleEnglish,

    /// Short description â€” 1 line, shopkeeper's register.
    @Default('') String description,

    /// SKU IDs in shopkeeper-curated order. Per PRD B1.4 AC #2 + UX Spec
    /// Â§4.3, the customer sees exactly these SKUs in this order. No
    /// pagination, no "load more" â€” the list is finite by design. Max
    /// 6 per UX Spec Â§4.3.
    @Default(<String>[]) List<String> skuIdsInOrder,

    required DateTime createdAt,
    DateTime? updatedAt,

    /// Soft-active flag. A shortlist can exist but be hidden from the
    /// customer (e.g., during seasonal re-curation).
    @Default(true) bool isActive,
  }) = _CuratedShortlist;

  /// JSON round-trip for Firestore serialization.
  factory CuratedShortlist.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
      _$CuratedShortlistFromJson(json);

  const CuratedShortlist._();

  /// True iff this shortlist has been curated with at least one SKU.
  /// Empty shortlists render the "à¤…à¤­à¥€ à¤¤à¤• à¤¸à¥à¤¨à¥€à¤² à¤­à¥ˆà¤¯à¤¾ à¤¨à¥‡ à¤‡à¤¸à¤®à¥‡à¤‚ à¤•à¥à¤› à¤¨à¤¹à¥€à¤‚ à¤šà¥à¤¨à¤¾"
  /// empty state per UX Spec Â§5.5 #27.
  bool get isCurated => skuIdsInOrder.isNotEmpty;

  /// True iff this shortlist has hit the 6-SKU finite cap from UX Spec Â§4.3.
  /// The shopkeeper curation UI (PRD B1.12) uses this to decide whether
  /// to show the "add from inventory" affordance.
  bool get atFiniteCap => skuIdsInOrder.length >= 6;
}

/// S4.19 â€” Shop deactivation flow.
class ShopDeactivationScreen extends ConsumerStatefulWidget {

      _ShopDeactivationScreenState();
}

class _ShopDeactivationScreenState

  // Step 1: AC #3 â€” reason selection

      mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
      children: [
        Icon(Icons.power_settings_new, color: YugmaColors.commit, size: 64),
        const SizedBox(height: YugmaSpacing.s4),

            fontSize: YugmaTypeScale.h3,
            fontWeight: FontWeight.w700,
            color: YugmaColors.commit,
          ),

        Text(
          'à¤µà¤œà¤¹: $_selectedReason',

            fontSize: YugmaTypeScale.body,
            color: YugmaColors.textSecondary,

        Text(
          strings.shopClosureReversibilityFooter,
          textAlign: TextAlign.center,
          style: TextStyle(
            fontFamily: YugmaFonts.devaBody,
            fontSize: YugmaTypeScale.caption,
            color: YugmaColors.textMuted,
          ),
        ),
        const SizedBox(height: YugmaSpacing.s8),
        SizedBox(
          width: double.infinity,
          height: YugmaSpacing.s12,
          child: ElevatedButton(
            onPressed: _processing ? null : _executeDeactivation,
            style: ElevatedButton.styleFrom(
              backgroundColor: YugmaColors.commit,
              foregroundColor: YugmaColors.textOnPrimary,
              textStyle: TextStyle(
                fontFamily: YugmaFonts.devaBody,
                fontWeight: FontWeight.w600,
              ),
              shape: RoundedRectangleBorder(
                borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(YugmaRadius.md),
              ),
            ),
            child: _processing
                ? SizedBox(
                    width: 24,
                    height: 24,
                    child: CircularProgressIndicator(
                      strokeWidth: 2,
                      color: YugmaColors.textOnPrimary,
                    ),
                  )
                : const Text('à¤¹à¤¾à¤, à¤¬à¤‚à¤¦ à¤•à¥€à¤œà¤¿à¤'),
          ),
        ),
        const SizedBox(height: YugmaSpacing.s3),
        TextButton(
          onPressed: () => Navigator.of(context).pop(),
          child: Text(
            'à¤¨à¤¹à¥€à¤‚, à¤µà¤¾à¤ªà¤¸ à¤œà¤¾à¤‡à¤',
            style: TextStyle(
              fontFamily: YugmaFonts.devaBody,
              color: YugmaColors.textMuted,
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ],
    );
  }

  Widget _bulletPoint(String text) {
    return Padding(
      padding: const EdgeInsets.only(bottom: YugmaSpacing.s2),
      child: Row(
        crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
        children: [
          Text('â€¢ ', style: TextStyle(color: YugmaColors.commit, fontSize: 16)),
          Expanded(
            child: Text(
              text,
              style: TextStyle(
                fontFamily: YugmaFonts.devaBody,
                fontSize: YugmaTypeScale.body,
                color: YugmaColors.textPrimary,
              ),
            ),
          ),
        ],
      ),
    );
  }

  // AC #5: write Shop lifecycle + AC #7: audit trail
  Future<void> _executeDeactivation() async {
    setState(() => _processing = true);
    final shopId = ref.read(shopIdProviderProvider).shopId;

    try {
      await FirebaseFirestore.instance
          .collection('shops')
          .doc(shopId)
          .set(<String, dynamic>{
        'shopLifecycle': 'deactivating',
        'shopLifecycleChangedAt': FieldValue.serverTimestamp(),
        'shopLifecycleReason': _selectedReason,
        'dpdpRetentionUntil': Timestamp.fromDate(
          DateTime.now().add(const Duration(days: 180)),
        ),
      }, SetOptions(merge: true));

      if (mounted) {
        ScaffoldMessenger.of(context).showSnackBar(
          SnackBar(content: Text('à¤¦à¥à¤•à¤¾à¤¨ à¤¬à¤‚à¤¦ à¤¹à¥‹à¤¨à¥‡ à¤•à¥€ à¤ªà¥à¤°à¤•à¥à¤°à¤¿à¤¯à¤¾ à¤¶à¥à¤°à¥‚ à¤¹à¥à¤ˆ')),
        );
        Navigator.of(context).pop();
      }
    } catch (e) {
      if (mounted) {
        ScaffoldMessenger.of(context).showSnackBar(
          SnackBar(content: Text(e.toString())),
        );
      }
    } finally {
      if (mounted) setState(() => _processing = false);
    }
  }
}

// =============================================================================
// Project â€” Freezed model for /shops/{shopId}/projects/{projectId}.
//
// Schema per SAD v1.0.4 Â§5, Project section, with Â§9 v1.0.4 field-partition
// table annotations. Every field is tagged with its partition owner in the
// doc comment so the sealed-union patches in `project_patch.dart` stay in
// sync without drift.
// =============================================================================

import 'package:freezed_annotation/freezed_annotation.dart';

import 'line_item.dart';

part 'project.freezed.dart';
part 'project.g.dart';

/// Project state machine â€” owned by operator (cannot be written by customer
/// except for the special `draft â†’ cancelled` transition, gated by rule).
enum ProjectState {
  @JsonValue('draft')
  draft,

  @JsonValue('negotiating')
  negotiating,

  @JsonValue('committed')
  committed,

  @JsonValue('paid')
  paid,

  @JsonValue('delivering')
  delivering,

  @JsonValue('closed')
  closed,

  @JsonValue('awaiting_verification')
  awaitingVerification,

  @JsonValue('cancelled')
  cancelled,
}

/// The Project document.
///
/// Field partition (SAD Â§9 v1.0.4):
///   Customer-owned: `occasion`, `unreadCountForCustomer`
///   Operator-owned: `state`, `totalAmount`, `amountReceivedByShop`,
///                   `lineItems`, `committedAt`, `paidAt`, `deliveredAt`,
///                   `closedAt`, `customerDisplayName`, `customerPhone`,
///                   `customerVpa`, `decisionCircleId`, `udhaarLedgerId`,
///                   `unreadCountForShopkeeper`
///   System-owned:   `lastMessagePreview`, `lastMessageAt`, `updatedAt`
///                   (Cloud Function writes only)
///
/// Enforcement is at compile-time via the three sealed-union patch classes
/// in `project_patch.dart`. Repository methods accept ONLY typed patches,
/// never `Map<String, dynamic>`. See PRD Standing Rule 11.
@freezed
class Project with _$Project {
  const factory Project({
    // ---- Identity ----
    required String projectId,
    required String shopId,
    required String customerId,
    required String customerUid,

    // ---- Customer-owned fields (SAD Â§9 partition) ----

    /// Customer-owned. Set at Project creation, immutable thereafter.
    /// One of: `shaadi`, `griha_pravesh`, `tyohaar`, `hadiya`, `dowry`, `other`.
    String? occasion,

    /// Customer-owned. Reset to 0 on open by the customer.
    @Default(0) int unreadCountForCustomer,

    // ---- Operator-owned fields (SAD Â§9 partition) ----

    /// Operator-owned. State machine transitions are gated by security rules
    /// (special-cased `draft â†’ cancelled` by customer is allowed via
    /// `ProjectCustomerCancelPatch` per I6.12 edge case #1).
    @Default(ProjectState.draft) ProjectState state,

    /// Operator-owned. Negotiated total in INR (paise omitted for v1 â€”
    /// almirah prices are round rupees at this scale).
    @Default(0) int totalAmount,

    /// Operator-owned. **Triple Zero invariant (C3.4 AC #4 + C3.5 AC #8):**
    /// must equal `totalAmount` at the `paid` state. Customer cannot write.
    /// Enforced at CI by cross-tenant integrity test.
    @Default(0) int amountReceivedByShop,

    /// Operator-owned. Denormalized line items for offline reads.
    @Default(<LineItem>[]) List<LineItem> lineItems,

    /// Operator-owned. Set when customer enters phone OTP at commit.
    DateTime? committedAt,
    DateTime? paidAt,
    DateTime? deliveredAt,
    DateTime? closedAt,

    /// Operator-owned. Read from customer_memory or UPI intent return.
    String? customerDisplayName,
    String? customerPhone,
    String? customerVpa,

    /// Operator-owned. Links to the Pariwar pillar (feature-flagged).
    String? decisionCircleId,

    /// Operator-owned. Payment method chosen by customer at checkout.
    /// One of: "upi", "cod", "bank_transfer", "udhaar". Null for draft.
    String? paymentMethod,

    /// Operator-owned. Links to the ADR-010 accounting mirror.
    String? udhaarLedgerId,

    /// Operator-owned. Reset to 0 on open by any operator.
    @Default(0) int unreadCountForShopkeeper,

    // ---- System-owned fields (Cloud Function writes only) ----

    /// System-owned. Short preview of the most recent chat message for
    /// Project list rendering without an extra read.
    String? lastMessagePreview,

    /// System-owned. Monotonically advances via transactional get + set in
    /// the new-message Cloud Function (SAD Â§9 invariant #3).
    DateTime? lastMessageAt,

    /// System-owned. Server timestamp on every write regardless of actor.
    DateTime? updatedAt,

    // ---- Timestamps (immutable after create) ----
    required DateTime createdAt,
  }) = _Project;

  factory Project.fromJson(Map<String, dynamic> json) =>
      _$ProjectFromJson(json);

  const Project._();

  /// True iff `amountReceivedByShop == totalAmount`. Required invariant at
  /// the `paid` state (Triple Zero, zero-commission math, CI-enforced).
  bool get zeroCommissionSatisfied => amountReceivedByShop == totalAmount;
}
